questions: Reforming capitalist society to achieve better rights for workers is desirable.
Revolution is the best way of achieving a socialist society.
The negative consequences of a revolution generally outweigh the advantages.
Liberal democracy is a viable way of achieving a socialist society.
A socialist revolution is made inevitable by the conditions of capitalism.
Workplace democracy within capitalism is an acceptable alternative to a complete socialization of the economy.
Revolutionary violence is acceptable as long as the final outcome is positive.
Redistributing wealth away from the wealthy through taxes is a viable way of defeating inequality.
Modern social democracy is a betrayal of left-wing values.
Oppressed peoples have the right to engage in a violent uprising when all other options have been exhausted.
The means of production, such as factories and farms, must be publicly owned.
Material conditions and needs are the dominant drive behind socioeconomic changes.
Socialism can only be fully achieved in developed industrialized societies.
''From each according to their ability, to each according to their needs'' is a good principle.
It is possible to peacefully convince the ruling class to conform to a socialist society.
Capitalism will induce its own demise through contradictions that result in crisis.
Class conflict is a force that has influenced much of human history.
The establishment of socialism is a gradual process, rather than an instant or rapid one.
Society is chiefly driven by individuals and ideas.
Total economic and social equality is realistically possible to achieve.
An economy is generally designed better when it is organized bottom-up rather than top-down.
Local planners, rather than national planners, are more efficient at running a planned economy.
A centrally planned economy based around computers is a concept worth investigating.
In underdeveloped societies a centrally planned economy is the best way to make rapid progress.
The principles of workplace democracy and self-management are desirable.
The state can be best defined as a monopoly on violence and oppression.
A highly centralized planned economy is not socialism but rather state capitalism.
Bureaucracy and inefficiency are always inherent in centrally planned economies.
It is necessary to establish a dictatorship of the proletariat as a transitionary stage between capitalism and socialism.
The government should be capable of assigning individuals to workplaces.
A world socialist republic is a realistic and desirable goal.
Nationalism and patriotism are impulses that are unacceptable in a socialist society.
The global socialist movement should be led by a single party or organization.
Foreign officials have no right to dictate policy in another country.
Open borders between likeminded socialist countries is desirable.
Any socialist country should be dedicated to exporting their ideology abroad.
People of similar cultures should unite in federations or confederations.
The international proletariat belongs to no country.
National liberation, or self determination for all peoples, is a good concept.
I would be willing to sacrifice economic resources in my home country in order to help other countries.
Socialist political parties should participate in liberal democratic elections.
Trade unions and workers councils should form the basis of a socialist society.
The leadership of political parties in the progression towards socialism will always lead to authoritarianism.
Democratic centralism is an authoritarian organizational structure that disregards the masses.
Only a mass workers party can achieve any meaningful long-term goals.
Mass spontaneous actions are more effective than carefully planned actions.
The fact that so many workers vote for bourgeois parties over socialist parties is proof that party politics are no longer relevant.
Socialist organizations are generally better off when organized loosely and decentrally.
Trade unionism has been largely corrupted by the ruling class and is no longer a viable structure for a socialist organization.
Climate change is a major global threat that all socialists must fiercely combat.
Measures to address environmental issues are unacceptable if they result in significant decreases in production and quality of life.
It is foolish to expect renewable energy sources to be able to replace fossil fuels.
Industrialized farming practices must be abolished even if it leads to lower outputs.
It will be important to implement extensive environmental protections as part of achieving socialism.
Experimental environmentally-friendly food sources like cultured meat are worth investing in.
Personal motor vehicles, such as cars, should be replaced with free public transport.
It is acceptable for humanity to suffer to some notable extent in order to preserve the natural ecosystem.
We must radically alter our food consumption in order to limit the exploitation of nature.
Human population growth must be curbed to prevent an ecological disaster.
Some small scale destruction of nature is acceptable if it notably benefits humanity.
Humanity must return to the primitive ways of the past to overcome long-term climate issues.
We must accept that the socialist states of the 20th century failed to seriously address environmental issues.
The oppression of LGBTQ+ people is a major issue that needs to be seriously addressed.
Traditional gender roles, such as women being homemakers, need to be overcome.
Marriage is a patriarchal social construct that should be phased out.
Abortion is an immoral act that should be banned or significantly limited.
Religions overall have a mostly positive effect and should remain influential in society.
Minority populations should receive special legal protections.
A socialist government has no right to disrupt religious or cultural traditions in any situation.
Prisons are oppressive and antiquated institutions that need to be abolished.
Policies that enable mass immigration are naïve and should not be implemented.
You cannot achieve a socialist society without also making significant social progress.
